description: Alex Chalk KC was Lord Chancellor and Secretary of State for Justice between 21 April 2023 and 5 July 2024. He was Minister of State in the Ministry of Defence from October 2022 to April 2023 and was formerly HM Solicitor General for England and Wales and the Prisons and Probation Minister. Alex Chalk KC was the MP for Cheltenham from 2015 to 2024. Prior to being elected in 2015, Alex was a barrister and prosecuted and defended in cases concerning terrorism, international fraud, and homicide. As Justice Minister, Alex led the Domestic Abuse Bill through the Commons. For the first time in history, the Bill includes a wide-ranging legal definition of domestic abuse which incorporates a range of abuses beyond physical violence, including emotional, coercive or controlling behaviour, and economic abuse. During his time as Prisons Minister, Alex announced a new scheme to provide temporary, basic accommodation to prison leavers for twelve weeks as well as overhauling the unpaid work that offenders are ordered to do as part of community service. As HM Solicitor General, Alex oversaw the work of the Law Officers’ Departments which includes the Crown Prosecution Service and Serious Fraud Office, as well as the Government Legal Department and HM Crown Prosecution Service Inspectorate. Alex lives in Cheltenham with his wife and three young children. Photo credit: Parliament.

details.body:
Alex Chalk KC was Lord Chancellor and Secretary of State for Justice between 21 April 2023 and 5 July 2024. He was Minister of State in the Ministry of Defence from October 2022 to April 2023 and was formerly HM Solicitor General for England and Wales and the Prisons and Probation Minister. Alex Chalk KC was the MP for Cheltenham from 2015 to 2024.

Prior to being elected in 2015, Alex was a barrister and prosecuted and defended in cases concerning terrorism, international fraud, and homicide.

As Justice Minister, Alex led the Domestic Abuse Bill through the Commons. For the first time in history, the Bill includes a wide-ranging legal definition of domestic abuse which incorporates a range of abuses beyond physical violence, including emotional, coercive or controlling behaviour, and economic abuse.

During his time as Prisons Minister, Alex announced a new scheme to provide temporary, basic accommodation to prison leavers for twelve weeks as well as overhauling the unpaid work that offenders are ordered to do as part of community service.

As HM Solicitor General, Alex oversaw the work of the Law Officers’ Departments which includes the Crown Prosecution Service and Serious Fraud Office, as well as the Government Legal Department and HM Crown Prosecution Service Inspectorate.

Alex lives in Cheltenham with his wife and three young children.

Photo credit: Parliament.

details.body:
Alex Chalk KC was Lord Chancellor and Secretary of State for Justice between 21 April 2023 and 5 July 2024. For the first time in history, the Bill includes a wide-ranging legal definition of domestic abuse which incorporates a range of abuses beyond physical violence, including emotional, coercive or controlling behaviour, and economic abuse.

During his time as Prisons Minister, Alex announced a new scheme to provide temporary, basic accommodation to prison leavers for twelve weeks as well as overhauling the unpaid work that offenders are ordered to do as part of community service.

image.alt_text: The Rt Hon Alex Chalk KC

person.title: The Rt Hon Alex Chalk KC

details.body:
• Oversight of all justice portfolios, policy, and Ministry of Justice strategy
  • Oversight of cross-cutting emergency  issues, responses  supported by other  ministers according to  portfolio
  • Oversight of international business and future relations with the EU 
  • MOJ support for the Union
  • Resourcing of the department 
  • Functions of the Lord Chancellor 
  • Judicial policy including pay, pensions and  diversity (these and  other operational  decisions affecting the  judiciary are reserved to the Lord Chancellor)

role.title: Lord Chancellor and Secretary of State for Justice

person.title: The Rt Hon Alex Chalk KC

details.body:
The Minister of State for Defence Procurement’s responsibilities include:

  • Defence procurement; Air, Land, Maritime and Joint domain
  • Complex Weapons
  • Trade, Exports, Foreign Investments and Economic Security
  • Defence and Security Industrial Strategy (DSIS)
  • Defence Industry Relationships
  • Equipment Plan
  • Defence Digital
  • Nuclear Enterprise
  • Shipbuilding Programmes (MOD)
  • Science and Technology (Dstl)
  • R&D and Innovation
  • Defence Estates
  • Defence Infrastructure Organisation (DIO)
  • Defence Estate Optimisation Programme
  • Service Family and Single Living Accommodation
  • Public Sector Land Release
  • Defence Suppliers Forum
  • Trade Associations
  • Commercial business

role.title: Minister of State (Minister for Defence Procurement)

role_appointments.title: Alex Chalk KC MP - Minister of State (Minister for Defence Procurement)

Alex Chalk KC was Lord Chancellor and Secretary of State for Justice between 21 April 2023 and 5 July 2024. image.alt_text: The Rt Hon Alex Chalk KC

person.title: The Rt Hon Alex Chalk KC

details.body:
The Solicitor General supports the Attorney across a range of responsibilities. This includes:

  • Responsibility for matters delegated by the Attorney General, including deputising for the Attorney at the Parliamentary Business and Legislation Cabinet Committee
  • Providing support to the Attorney General in his superintendence of the Government Legal Department, the Crown Prosecution Service, HM Crown Prosecution Service Inspectorate and the Serious Fraud Office
  • Supporting the Attorney General in his public interest functions
  • Promoting the rule of law at home and internationally
